programmes.show_description: VW Beetle / Royal Enfield Cycar: The team think they can smell profit by choosing one of the simplest cars ever built, the VW Beetle.
AA Bikes/MGA/Terrapin: Derek picks up a quartet of tasty vehicles from a farm in Shropshire. Among them a 1937 Austin Seven van and a Triumph TR2 from the mid-50s.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien look for valuable items languishing in people's homes, unearthing a church pew, create a fish tank from scrap and uncover a special petrol tank.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Cat Among the Pigeons: Lucy Worsley explores Agatha Christie's haunted, unconventional early life to discover the origins of her talent for murder.
Shopping from Home.
Dagenham to Battersea: Michael Portillo is in London, where he tracks the River Thames from east to west. Starting in Dagenham, he ends up at the iconic Battersea Power Station.
Park Royal To Westminster: Michael Portillo discovers the origins of Harry Beck's map of the London Underground, and explores the headquarters of Churchill's war cabinet.
Urmston to New Islington: Michael Portillo is in Greater Manchester, hearing about the birth of the NHS and the first episode of Coronation Street.
Oldham to Wakefield: Michael Portillo visits Oldham's Victorian theatre, attends a knife-making workshop in Sheffield and goes to the Yorkshire Sculpture Park near Wakefield.
Cortina/Granada: Dave travels to leafy Leicestershire where Steve Perry has a 1980 Ford Granada Ghia and a 1981 Ford Cortina.

Lotus Esprit/Land Rover: Derek heads to County Durham where Neil Walker is reluctantly letting his late dad's car collection go, including a Japanese take on a classic British Jag; the Mitsuoka Viewt.
Barbarossa: A milestone in TV history: Laurence Olivier tells the story of WWII. Hitler turns his tanks towards Russia. After a succession of victories, the Germans delay fatally.
In blacked-out London four young women are murdered - just like Jack fifty years before. When two flights disappear without a trace, the legend of the Bermuda Triangle begins.
Wollaton Hall 2: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wollaton Hall in Nottingham, where items brought in include a portrait of a forgotten Romantic poet.
Toys & Childhood Special: A special episode about the toys we love and how they've shaped our childhood, from Sindy and Action Man to Subbuteo and Lego.
Mazda MX5 / Moto Guzzi V50: The team opt to buy one of the most affordable sports cars on the road - the Mazda MX-5.
VW T25 / Audi TT Quattro: The team pay over the odds for a VW Campervan, but can they hope to break even when they turn out a lovely bus for auction.
Barbarossa: A milestone in TV history: Laurence Olivier tells the story of WWII. Hitler turns his tanks towards Russia. After a succession of victories, the Germans delay fatally.
In blacked-out London four young women are murdered - just like Jack fifty years before. When two flights disappear without a trace, the legend of the Bermuda Triangle begins.
Wollaton Hall 2: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Wollaton Hall in Nottingham, where items brought in include a portrait of a forgotten Romantic poet.
Simon Reeve embarks on the third leg of his four-part journey around the Mediterranean, travelling through Libya, Tunisia and across the sea to Sicily.
Riley Falcon: Dave's in Lincolnshire picking up a 1935 Riley Falcon - a good-looking saloon car, with a pre-select gearbox.
Willys Jeep/Austin A35/Case Tractor/Hummer: Derek picks up some very special working vehicles. Charlie feels the pull of a gargantuan Hummer and Paul extracts a vintage tractor from a Yorkshire barn.
Mazda MX5 / Moto Guzzi V50: The team opt to buy one of the most affordable sports cars on the road - the Mazda MX-5.
VW T25 / Audi TT Quattro: The team pay over the odds for a VW Campervan, but can they hope to break even when they turn out a lovely bus for auction.
Cortina/Granada: Dave travels to leafy Leicestershire where Steve Perry has a 1980 Ford Granada Ghia and a 1981 Ford Cortina.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien are in East Sussex and Hertfordshire, with finds that include a Victorian egg incubator.

programmes.show_name: Agatha Christie: Lucy Worsley on the Mystery Queen
programmes.show_description: Destination Unknown: Lucy Worsley examines Agatha's mysterious disappearance in December 1926 and reveals the influence this episode had on her writing.
Shopping from Home.
Wakefield to Leeds: In Wakefield, West Yorkshire, Michael Portillo visits the National Coal Mining Museum for England at Caphouse Colliery.
Bradford to Hebdon Bridge: Michael Portillo continues his post-war exploration of northwest England in Bradford and Shipley, before ending at Hebden Bridge.
Tilbury to Barbican: Michael Portillo travels from London's Docklands to the Barbican, examining the story of the arrival at Tilbury in 1948 of over a thousand passengers from the Caribbean.
Waterloo to Regent's Park: Michael Portillo visits London's South Bank, where the 1951 Festival of Britain drew more than eight and a half million visitors.
Ford Fiesta/Malton Forest Rally: Dave collects a Ford Fiesta XR2i that's not moved from a garage for twenty-one years and Derek picks up a 1956 Siva Tourer, made famous in Doctor Who.
Robin Reliant/Triumphs/Bantam: Dave meets the inspirational owner of a Reliant Robin who's selling it to raise money for charity.
Banzai!: December 7, 1941 saw the infamous Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor. Laurence Olivier revisits this pivotal moment of World War II in the much-praised documentary series.
The Germans hear that Polish forces are fighting alongside a huge bear. Tales of this beer-swilling, chain-smoking beast terrify them - but incredibly the stories are true.
Nursing Special: Fiona Bruce presents an edition dedicated to nursing, with objects providing an insight into the history of the profession.
Eden Project 1: Fiona Bruce presents an episode filmed before the Accession, featuring the Queen Consort's visit to the Eden Project to discuss charity project The Big Lunch.

Mini Cooper Mk2 / MGTF: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window.
Morgan / Raleigh Super Grifter: The Restoring Classics team set out on a Pre-War challenge with a 1939 three wheeled Morgan F4 - and aptly named Russ Storer from MG Mecca is still keen to take it on.
Banzai!: December 7, 1941 saw the infamous Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor. Laurence Olivier revisits this pivotal moment of World War II in the much-praised documentary series.
The Germans hear that Polish forces are fighting alongside a huge bear. Tales of this beer-swilling, chain-smoking beast terrify them - but incredibly the stories are true.
Nursing Special: Fiona Bruce presents an edition dedicated to nursing, with objects providing an insight into the history of the profession.
Simon Reeve embarks on the final leg of his journey around the Mediterranean, travelling from Morocco, across the sea to Spain, and on to Corsica and the French Riviera.
Giant Royal Locomotive: Expert hauliers Eric Harrison and Chris Case move The Royal Highland Fusilier halfway across the country to the Great Central Railway's Diesel Gala in Loughborough.
David Jason operates one of the world's biggest hydraulic cranes, gets behind the wheel of a tank, and discovers the story behind the invention of the hovercraft.

Mini Cooper Mk2 / MGTF: A 1969 Morris Mini Cooper MkII is totally restored by specialists. They give it the full works, which means only one thing... any chance of profit heads towards an open window.
